visa_policy.notes: Visa-free list sourced from the official Moroccan Ministry of Foreign Affairs consular portal (consulat.ma). The list is in French; country names have been translated to English. Most countries have no explicit maximum stay stated; only specific exceptions are noted verbatim (Dominican Republic 60 days, Hong Kong 30 days, Hungary 90 days, Malaysia 90 days, Maldives 30 days, Kazakhstan 30 days/180-day period, Azerbaijan 90 days, Ghana 90 days). Congo (Brazzaville), Ecuador, Ghana, and Guinea require a permanent AEVM (Electronic Travel Authorization) from acces-maroc.ma prior to arrival even though they are visa-free. Several African countries (Algeria, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Gabon, Mali, Niger, Senegal, Togo, Tunisia) had a temporary AEVM requirement introduced 25 September 2025 for AFCON 2025, which was lifted on 25 January 2026; Mali's AEVM was additionally lifted on 27 April 2026. Ivory Coast (CIV) had visa exemption cancelled on 1 September 2024 and now requires a visa. New visa-free countries added since last record: Cape Verde (8 May 2024), Azerbaijan (28 August 2024), Kazakhstan (19 March 2025), Ghana (6 June 2025, with AEVM), Ecuador (1 August 2025, with AEVM). The e-visa was launched on 10 July 2022 via the official acces-maroc.ma portal. The e-visa duration is 30 days, extendable up to 6 months with multiple entries; processing time is 24-72 Moroccan working hours. New e-visa nationalities added in January 2023: India, Jordan, Guatemala (confirmed official). Additional Category A nationalities (Benin, Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Vietnam) confirmed from Wikipedia/secondary sources but individual official announcement URLs not recovered.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit of an EU member state, valid minimum 180 days from application date

conditional_access.conditions: Foreigners subject to visa formality holding a valid ordinary passport and a residence permit of one of the EU countries, valid for a minimum of 180 days from the date of application. E-visa valid 30 days, extendable up to 6 months with multiple entries. Processing 24-72 hours. Apply via www.acces-maroc.ma

Visa-free entry for holders of diplomatic, service, special, and/or official passports (specific passport types vary by country) per bilateral visa abolition agreements signed between Morocco and each listed country. Full per-country breakdown of covered passport types available at the official source URL. The specific passport type covered for each country is documented in the official table (diplomatic / service / special / official columns).

Agreement signed 11 February 2026 in Addis Ababa by Minister Nasser Bourita and Comorian FM Mbae Mohamed on the sidelines of the 48th Ordinary Session of the AU Executive Council. Nationals holding diplomatic or service passports valid for at least six months may enter, stay, exit, and transit without a visa for a period not exceeding 90 days within a 180-day period from the date of first entry. Diplomatic mission members and their household family members holding diplomatic or service passports may stay without visa for the entire period of accreditation.
Mutual memorandum of understanding on entry visa exemption for holders of diplomatic passports, signed 8 February 2025 at the headquarters of the Moroccan Foreign Ministry between Iraq and Morocco.

cbi.notes: Morocco does not offer a citizenship-by-investment program. No official Moroccan government source (diplomatie.ma, consulat.ma, acces-maroc.ma) establishes any such program. All search results referencing CBI in relation to Morocco are from non-official aggregator or law firm sites, and those sites also confirm Morocco does not have a CBI program.

programs.name: Moroccan Residence Permit — Investor Category (Certificat d'Immatriculation)
programs.notes: An investor visa category exists within the ordinary visa framework as documented on the official Moroccan consular portal. Applicants require a recommendation from the Management of External Investments, a professional federation, a proper authority for supervision, Regional Centers of Investment (C.R.I.), or a Moroccan partner/foreign investor branch in Morocco. No minimum investment threshold amount is stated on the official source. After entry, foreigners staying more than 90 days must apply for a Certificat d'Immatriculation (residence permit) from the Bureau des Etrangers. This is a conventional residence framework, not a formal golden visa program with published financial thresholds.

validation_notes: REMOVED Ivory Coast (CIV) from visa_free: visa exemption for ordinary passport holders was cancelled effective 1 September 2024 following Cote d'Ivoire imposing visa requirements linked to irregular migration concerns.
ADDED Cape Verde (CPV) to visa_free: ordinary passport visa exemption in force since 8 May 2024; a temporary AEVM requirement for AFCON 2025 (25 Sep 2025 – 25 Jan 2026) has been lifted.
ADDED Azerbaijan (AZE) to visa_free: visa exemption agreement entered into force 28 August 2024, 90-day stay.
ADDED Kazakhstan (KAZ) to visa_free: visa exemption agreement effective 19 March 2025, 30 days per 180-day period.
ADDED Ghana (GHA) to visa_free: bilateral visa waiver effective 6 June 2025, up to 90 days, but AEVM (Electronic Travel Authorization) permanently required via acces-maroc.ma.
ADDED Ecuador (ECU) to visa_free: visa-free resumed 1 August 2025, but AEVM permanently required via acces-maroc.ma.
UPDATED Mali (MLI) notes: AEVM requirement lifted 27 April 2026; Mali is now plain visa-free with no AEVM needed.
UPDATED Burkina Faso, Niger, Senegal, Togo, Tunisia, Gabon, Algeria notes: temporary AEVM required 25 Sep 2025 – 25 Jan 2026 for AFCON 2025 security measures, then lifted; all now plain visa-free.
ADDED India (IND), Jordan (JOR), Guatemala (GTM) to e_visa (Category A): added per official diplomatie.ma statement effective 10 January 2023.

ADDED Benin (BEN), Brunei Darussalam (BRN), Cambodia (KHM), Laos (LAO), Vietnam (VNM) to e_visa (Category A): confirmed from Wikipedia/Fragomen secondary sources; direct official diplomatie.ma announcement URLs not recovered.
CORRECTED e_visa source_url for Thailand and Israel to use official diplomatie.ma launch page instead of India embassy page.
ADDED UAE as a conditional_access residence permit issuer (Category B e-visa) per Wikipedia/secondary sources.
UPDATED Congo (Brazzaville) and Guinea (Conakry) notes to clarify permanent AEVM status (not related to AFCON temporary measure).
gaps: Maximum stay duration not specified for the majority of visa-free countries on the official consulat.ma list; only 7 countries have explicit limits stated (Dominican Republic 60 days, Hong Kong 30 days, Hungary 90 days, Malaysia 90 days, Maldives 30 days, Kazakhstan 30 days/180-day period, Azerbaijan 90 days).

Official consulat.ma visa-free list page (consulat.ma/fr/...) is returning access errors (WAF rejection) during this validation; the list content was verified against the Wikipedia Visa policy of Morocco article and secondary sources but could not be read directly from the official page.
Full official announcement for e-visa expansion to Benin, Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Vietnam not found on diplomatie.ma directly; confirmed from Fragomen/Wikipedia secondary sources only.
Ghana and Ecuador ordinary-passport visa-free + AEVM entries use secondary sources (en.hespress.com, Wikipedia) as diplomatie.ma official announcement pages were inaccessible due to SSL errors.
The acces-maroc.ma portal is a JavaScript SPA; real-time eligibility list could not be scraped to enumerate all currently active e-visa nationalities.
Investor residence permit minimum investment amount not found on any official Moroccan government source; no golden visa or formal investment-threshold residency program exists.

Iraq diplomatic passport MoU (8 Feb 2025) sourced from Iraqi MFA (mofa.gov.iq); no corresponding announcement confirmed on Moroccan diplomatie.ma — recorded with source_official=true as mofa.gov.iq is an official government domain.
No official digital nomad, startup, talent, or fast-track visa program found on official Moroccan government sources.

Morocco is not a member of any formal freedom-of-movement bloc; the agreements array is empty because no official source confirms membership in ECOWAS, GCC, EU/EEA, Arab League free movement, or similar multilateral arrangements.
The diplomatic/official passport agreement table on consulat.ma does not specify agreement dates or maximum stay durations for most bilateral agreements.

outbound_official.notes: Official page of Morocco's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates (consulat.ma) listing countries where Moroccan ordinary passport holders may enter without a prior consular visa. Page is undated. The list is as scraped from the live page on 2026-06-14. Some entries carry conditions noted verbatim from the source (Egypt marriage condition, Jordan age restriction, Hong Kong/Philippines/Maldives/Sao Tome stay limits). The page does not separately categorize visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA; all listed destinations are presented as unconditional or conditionally visa-free. Morocco's official outbound list is notably shorter than aggregator indexes because it reflects only formally agreed exemptions, not reciprocal/unilateral grants.

sources_checked.description: Official Moroccan MFA consular portal — complete visa-exempt nationalities list (French). Returned WAF error during this validation; content verified against secondary sources.
Official Moroccan Ministry of Foreign Affairs — e-visa launch announcement (July 2022) with Category A/B details
Official Moroccan MFA — statement on new countries benefiting from e-visa effective 10 January 2023 (India, Jordan, Guatemala confirmed)
Official Moroccan MFA — announcement of Morocco-Kazakhstan visa exemption agreement effective 19 March 2025
Official Moroccan MFA — Cape Verde-Morocco cooperation agreements including ordinary passport visa exemption
Official Moroccan Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Visiting Morocco page
Official MAECI portal for eVisa and AEVM applications (JavaScript SPA; eligibility form not scrapable)
Official Moroccan MFA consular portal — complete list of countries with bilateral agreements for visa abolition for holders of official/diplomatic/service passports
Official Moroccan MFA diplomatie.ma — press release on Morocco-Comoros visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders, signed 11 February 2026
Official Iraqi Ministry of Foreign Affairs — announcement of Morocco-Iraq mutual MoU on visa exemption for diplomatic passport holders, signed 8 February 2025

Wikipedia Visa policy of Morocco — used to cross-check recent policy changes (2024-2026) including Ivory Coast cancellation, new visa-free countries, AEVM requirements, and e-visa expansion. Not an official source; used for gap-filling only.
Hespress (Moroccan news) — Ghana visa-free with AEVM from 6 June 2025. Used as secondary source; Hespress is a major Moroccan news outlet citing official sources.
